$expand.name: go-fleet-metrics-hub-children
$expand.why: go-fleet-metrics-hub is a compose project shipping three independently-addressable services (discovery http_sd, prometheus, grafana). Each gets its own slug + host_port + *.0exec.com domain today; if the children later split into separate repos they keep the same slugs and only repo_url changes. Triggered by a real port-allocation collision on 2026-05-18 (fleet-discovery had claimed 18201 on the dockerhost but was not in the registry, so allocate-port re-handed it out).

$external.description: Self-hosted Plausible Analytics (ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition). Third-party upstream container — its own docker-compose lives at /opt/services/plausible/ and is managed by hand (NOT by fleet-runner). Registered here so allocate-port and audit-port know host_port 18204 is claimed.
$external.name: Plausible Analytics
$external.why: Plausible is fleet-pixel's optional upstream (see overrides.json: fleet-pixel). Running unregistered on 2026-05-19 caused fleet-runner allocate-port to re-hand-out 18204; the subsequent fleet-persona deploy clobbered /opt/services/plausible/docker-compose.yml. Registering it is the metadata fix — runtime: external keeps fleet-runner deploy/build off the compose dir.

$rules.name: proxy-egress-by-category
$rules.why: Active scanners + scrapers must egress via Webshare (FLEET.md §6). `domains` joined 2026-05-19 to cover 0crawl domain-scanners (a11y-quick, broken-links, …) that probe bug-bounty targets and were carrying proxy_egress=true via legacy hand-edit. Audit `infrastructure`/`content`/`proxy` separately — those have mixed external/internal callers and need per-slug overrides, not a category rule.

$rules.name: proxy-egress-external-scanners-by-id
$rules.why: Per-id pin for scanners whose category isn't covered by `proxy-egress-by-category` (infrastructure / content) but which actively probe external bug-bounty targets per FLEET.md §6. Operator to migrate these into a proper category over time and retire this id list.

$rules.name: wildcard-cert-0exec
$rules.why: All *.0exec.com vhosts share /etc/letsencrypt/live/wildcard.0exec.com/. Issued via Hetzner DNS-01; renewed by certbot timer.

$rules.name: wildcard-cert-0crawl
$rules.why: All *.0crawl.com vhosts share /etc/letsencrypt/live/wildcard.0crawl.com/. Issued via Hetzner DNS-01 on 2026-05-18; renewed by certbot timer.

admin-finder.trl_ceiling_reason: Authenticated admin discovery requires site-specific credentials or session state.
admin-finder.trl_evidence:
v1.6.0 — TRL 5→6 uplift.
- Bundled curated wordlist (~150 paths from SecLists + OWASP CMS + vendor docs)
  via go:embed; deduped, sorted, comment-stripped at parse time.
- Per-probe: HEAD then GET fallback (for 405/400 and body sniffing on 2xx);
  follows up to 2 manual redirects; per-request 3s, total 60s, worker pool 8.
- Login-form sniffer (password / username / sign in / log in / type=password)
  with strong-signal short-circuit ("password" alone) and 2-of-N rule.
- First-hop Location preserved across follow chain so "302 to /wp-admin/login"
  classifies as present even when the followed target is 404.
- Optional ?wordlist_url= composes with sibling go-pentest-wordlists
  (cap 500 entries, 256 KiB fetch budget, SSRF-safe via go-common/safehttp).
- SCOPE_GUARD_URL env (comma list, supports *.example.com) gates which
  hosts may be probed; off when unset.
- Backward-compat: ?target= alias for ?url=.
- Tests: handler_test.go + classify_test.go + wordlist_test.go;
  httptest fake server covers 200-with-form / 302-to-login / 401 /
  200-generic / 302-to-home / 404. `go test ./...` green.

agent-approval.description: Human-in-the-loop approval gate. POST /request to submit a risky action for human sign-off; GET /await to block until decided or timed out; POST /decide to record the human decision. Timeout resolves to a configurable default (deny by default). The safe-autonomy keystone.

agent-mailbox.description: Addressed request/reply bus for agents: POST /send to enqueue a message for a named agent, GET /inbox to drain it, POST /reply to send a correlated reply, GET /await to block until a reply arrives. Closes the A→B→A gap that fleet-notify (broadcast) and fleet-q (anonymous pull) leave open.

agent-quorum.description: k-of-n answer aggregation and voting for redundant agents. Create a round, cast votes, GET /result for the aggregated decision (majority/weighted/unanimous). Productizes the adversarial-verify loop as a fleet primitive.

agent-tools.description: Turns the fleet catalog into an LLM function-calling manifest. GET /manifest returns a callable tool spec per service (name, description, base_url, auth, example_path). POST /refresh re-fetches the live catalog. Used by agents to discover how to call any fleet service.

analyze-headers.trl_evidence:
Full OWASP secure-header matrix: HSTS (max-age/includeSubDomains/preload),
CSP (parsed; flags unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval/wildcard/data:/http: in script-src
and style-src; checks default-src, object-src 'none', frame-ancestors), COOP,
COEP, CORP, Permissions-Policy (risky sinks parsed), Referrer-Policy,
X-Frame-Options (ALLOW-FROM deprecation), X-Content-Type-Options,
X-XSS-Protection (deprecated; flagged), Cache-Control, Server/X-Powered-By
information disclosure. SSRF-safe via go-common/safehttp; HEAD-first with
GET fallback when origin rejects HEAD. Composite grade is critical-aware:
any missing HSTS on HTTPS or missing CSP forces F. Split into headers.go,
csp.go, grade.go, handler.go; covered by handler_test.go (6 cases including
perfect-site, weak-CSP, missing-HSTS, deprecated-X-XSS) and csp_test.go (7
parser/grader cases). `go test ./...` green.

api-extractor.trl_evidence: v1.4.5 extracts API-endpoint surface from HTML/JS via static scan + BaaS-host gazetteer + auth-scheme inference + evidence trail. Root-cause FP: primary fetch was pinned to fleetfetch.RenderJS, routing every request through the down-fleet-wide chromedp render-cache, so 502s masqueraded as status=1/zero-endpoints (61.3% zero-rate on 1000 v1.4.3 rows). Switched to RenderDefault + WithFallbackOnTimeout. On 40 originally-502-blocked domains: before 40/40 hard-502+zero, after 36/40 (90%) honest 200 (3 recovered real endpoints, 33 confirmed true-negatives); github/stripe/vercel went 502->200 with 3/90/12 endpoints. Tests: TestCapUTF8RuneSafe + TestHandlerNonLatinBodyExtraction.

Does not persist to domainscope so sampling was live-probe over script-diverse real domains.

citation-reference-extractor.description: Extract academic and web citations from a page or raw HTML/text — <cite> elements, reference/bibliography list items, footnote/endnote targets, and structured identifiers (DOI, arXiv ids, checksum-validated ISBN-10/13, PMID, and reference-context URLs), normalised into typed citation objects with a by-type summary.
citation-reference-extractor.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over a fetched/pasted page (golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer, no regex over HTML structure): prunes script/style/noscript/svg/template/head/iframe subtrees, then runs four ordered passes — (1) reference/bibliography list items: <ol>/<ul> <li> contents inside a section whose heading text or container id/class matches a reference token (references / bibliography / works cited / literature cited / further reading); (2) every <cite> element's text; (3) footnote/endnote <li> targets keyed by id prefix (fn / footnote / endnote / note / cite_note / ref-) with the trailing number lifted into a marker; (4) an inline sweep for structured identifiers in running prose not already captured. Each citation's raw text is matched for identifiers with anchored regexes (pattern-matching within text, not HTML parsing): DOI (10.<4-9 digits>/<suffix>), arXiv new (NNNN.NNNNN[vN]) and old (archive.subclass/NNNNNNN) schemes normalised to arXiv:<id>, PMID (label-required), http/https URLs (DOI-resolver and arXiv URLs de-duplicated against their id forms), and ISBN-10/ISBN-13 validated by their mod-11 / mod-10 checksums (labelled and bare 978/979 forms; checksum-failing candidates dropped). Output is a typed citation list (raw text + detected type + extracted identifiers) plus a by-type + by-identifier summary. Input fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so many producers scanning the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode; 4 MiB body cap. SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode scans pasted content with no fetch. Per-failure notes pushed to a non-nil degraded[] (no_references, fetch_failed:<err>) instead of failing the response. ~30 unit + handler tests cover DOI extraction, both arXiv id forms, ISBN-10 and ISBN-13 valid + invalid checksums, PMID, <cite> extraction, reference-list detection by heading and by container id/class, URL-in-reference extraction, footnote markers, type classification, summary counts, parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, no-references→degraded, and text= mode. TRL-4 ceiling: heuristic section detection (no full CSL / citation-style parsing, no author/title/year field decomposition), English-centric reference-section headings, and no DOI/ISBN registry resolution (checksum validity only, not 'this identifier exists'). /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (DOI/arXiv parse, ISBN checksum, reference-list + <cite> DOM detection, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

color-contrast-checker.description: WCAG 2.x color-contrast checker for a web page or raw HTML — resolves declared foreground/background color pairs (inline style= and <style>-block rules), computes the relative-luminance contrast ratio for each, and reports AA/AAA pass/fail for normal and large text plus a failing-pairs summary.
color-contrast-checker.trl_evidence: Real WCAG 2.x contrast engine over a golang.org/x/net/html DOM walk (no regex over HTML structure): collects foreground/background color declarations from (1) inline style= attributes and (2) <style>-block CSS rules parsed by a small brace-matching tokenizer that strips /* */ comments and flattens one level of @media/@supports nesting. Colors are resolved by a curated ~148-entry CSS named-color map plus #rgb/#rrggbb (and #rgba/#rrggbbaa alpha-drop) hex and rgb()/rgba() functional notation (comma or space/slash separated, integer or percentage channels, alpha parsed-then-dropped). For each resolved fg+bg pair it computes the exact sRGB relative luminance (channel/255, piecewise <=0.03928 ? /12.92 : ((c+0.055)/1.055)^2.4; L = 0.2126R+0.7152G+0.0722B) and the contrast ratio (L_lighter+0.05)/(L_darker+0.05) — verified against the canonical black-on-white = 21.0 and white-on-white = 1.0. Verdicts use >= thresholds at the WCAG boundaries: AA normal 4.5, AAA normal 7, AA large 3, AAA large 4.5. Output is de-duplicated, sorted worst-ratio-first, with a summary (total pairs, # failing AA normal, skipped/unparseable color count, min ratio). Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so N producers checking the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode. SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode scores pasted HTML with no fetch. Per-failure notes pushed to a non-nil degraded[] (fetch_failed, no_color_pairs, unparseable_colors_skipped:<n>) instead of failing the response. Documented TRL-4 ceiling: it scores only directly-declared color pairs on the same element/rule — NO computed-style cascade, inheritance, or specificity resolution, so it does not reconstruct the fg/bg a browser would actually composite; no JS-applied styles; no opacity/alpha compositing; CSS variables, gradients, currentColor, and hsl() are reported as skipped rather than guessed. ~40 table-driven unit + handler tests cover luminance of black/white/mid-gray, the 21.0 and 1.0 anchor ratios, hex (#fff/#ffffff) and rgb()/rgba() parsing, named-color resolution, inline-style and <style>-block pair extraction, AA/AAA verdicts at the boundaries, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection (handler + parseInput), and degraded[] population on a no-colors page. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (luminance/ratio anchors, parsing, inline + stylesheet extraction, threshold verdicts, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

company-size.trl_ceiling_reason: Authoritative headcount needs a paid data provider (LinkedIn / Clearbit / Crunchbase). CPU-only static-HTML inference plateaus at principled multi-signal banding with confidence -- it cannot certify an exact employee count.
company-size.trl_evidence: Pass-3 geo: all headcount detection in v3.1.1 keyed on English phrasing only (employees, team of N, staff of N, headcount), so the entire non-English web returned band=unknown even when the count was in the fetched HTML -- confirmed live: siemens.com renders 'unserer Mitarbeiter 438', bosch.de '413.000 Mitarbeiter', sap.com mis-read as 1-10, lvmh.fr / telefonica.es / toyota.jp / alibaba.com / hyundai.com all band=unknown. v3.2.0 adds geo.go: a multilingual headcount gazetteer in 11 languages + 3 CJK scripts (Mitarbeiter/Beschaeftigte DE, salaries/effectif/collaborateurs FR, empleados/plantilla ES, funcionarios PT, dipendenti IT, medewerkers NL, pracownikow PL, sotrudnikov RU, JP/CN/KR), both noun-first and number-first orderings + 'emploie/zatrudnia N' equivalents, a locale-aware integer parser (12.500 / 12 500 / 12'500 / 1,200名 all parse correctly -- the old comma-only parser read '12.500 Mitarbeiter' as 12), and a multilingual employees-vs-customers guard (Kunden/clients/utilisateurs/users rejected). The multilingual count is an authoritative 0.9-weight band signal. A space-grouping false positive (siemens 'Mitarbeiter 438 438 Millionen' -> bogus 438438) was found mid-pass and fixed by a tight, whitespace-free number token on noun-first patterns. On the fetched-page non-English live set, detection went from 3/12 to 9/12 (sap mis-classification corrected). 88+ unit tests incl. 41 new Pass-3 cases (locale parser, 18-language/script detection, 10-language customer-rejection, siemens regression, multilingual disambiguator, German GmbH end-to-end through the HTTP handler), gofmt/vet/-race clean. Ceiling unchanged at 7: an exact certified headcount still needs a paid provider. Residual non-English unknowns are fetch-tier variance (bot-hostile sites returning 0 pages), not detector misses -- proven by re-probes alternating sigs=0/unknown with sigs=10/1000+ on the same domain.

compression-tester.trl_evidence: Pass-3 found the prior probe client used fleetfetch.NewHTTPClient(RenderDefault), which despite its name routes every GET through the shared Redis fetch-cache, silently ignoring Transport.DisableCompression and collapsing the five independent Accept-Encoding negotiations into one warm cached body. On 1000 real v1.6.1 rows this produced 344/1000 (34%) recording supports_gzip=false while supports_br=true (implausible; gzip is the universal fallback). Replaced with a direct *http.Transport using safehttp.GuardedDialer (same SSRF/DNS-rebind guard, no new dep) + DisableCompression=true + ProxyFromEnvironment, so each offer and the raw wire bytes reach the origin. Re-probed the FP cohort live against deployed v1.6.2: 18/20 (90%) now correctly report gzip honored, 2 are genuine true negatives. 27 unit tests green.

content-fingerprint.description: Near-duplicate-detection fingerprints for a web page or raw text — a 64-bit Charikar SimHash over k-gram word shingles, a 64-permutation MinHash signature for Jaccard estimation, and a normalized SHA-256 exact-match digest, plus shingle and token counts.
content-fingerprint.trl_evidence: Near-duplicate fingerprinting over a real HTML text extractor (golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer, no regex): prunes script/style/noscript/svg/template/head/iframe subtrees, prefers the first <article>/<main> region then falls back to <body>, collapses whitespace and inserts block-element boundaries so words aren't glued. Computes three standard fingerprints over the normalized lowercased text: a 64-bit Charikar SimHash over k=3 word shingles (FNV-64a per shingle, +1/-1 column sums, sign bit) — near-identical documents have a small Hamming distance, unrelated documents ~half the bits; a 64-permutation MinHash signature (universal-hash multiply-xor scramble with a splitmix64 avalanche per permutation, min per permutation) whose matching-fraction estimates Jaccard similarity (identical sets 1.0, disjoint ~0 within ~12% sampling error); and a whitespace-collapsed, lowercased SHA-256 hex exact-match digest. Shingle and token counts are returned alongside. A hammingDistanceHex helper documents how to compare two SimHash values. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so 20 producers fingerprinting the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode. SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode fingerprints pasted content with no fetch. Per-failure notes pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed, no_extractable_text, low_sample:<n>_tokens) instead of failing the response; <20-token inputs flagged as statistically unstable. ~30 unit tests cover SimHash determinism/case-insensitivity/empty, near-duplicate small-Hamming stability and unrelated large-Hamming divergence, the Hamming helper, MinHash signature length/determinism, Jaccard identical=1.0/disjoint~0/similarity-ordering/mismatched-length, normalized SHA-256 exact match plus case/whitespace insensitivity and content-change sensitivity, shingle generation at the k boundary/below-k/dedup, token+shingle counts, parse-input aliases, low-sample degraded, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, and text= mode. TRL-4 ceiling: fixed k=3 shingle width, no language-aware tokenization, FNV/universal-hash heuristics rather than cryptographic locality-sensitive guarantees, and fingerprints estimate surface/lexical similarity not semantic meaning. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (identical→identical fingerprints, one-word→small SimHash Hamming distance but different SHA, unrelated→large Hamming, MinHash Jaccard sanity, extraction, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

cookie-checker.trl_ceiling_reason: Header-only fetcher with no JS engine; a large share of CMP/consent cookies (OneTrust OptanonConsent, Cookiebot, most banners) are JS-set after the consent click and never appear in Set-Cookie headers, so a header-only audit structurally under-observes CMP cookies regardless of gazetteer breadth. Closing that needs a headless browser, out of scope for a CPU-only pure-Go service.
cookie-checker.trl_evidence: Live-probe Set-Cookie auditor (RFC 6265bis hand-written parser, per-cookie evidence trail, CMP/tracker/anti-bot gazetteer, real table-driven tests). Pass-3 closed a geographic-coverage bias: the Pass-1/2 gazetteer knew only Anglo-centric CMPs (OneTrust/Cookiebot/Quantcast/TrustArc) and fell through to class=other on the entire EU/non-US consent ecosystem. Added 15 EU/non-US CMPs + WordPress consent-plugin ecosystem (Complianz/Borlabs/CookieYes/Axeptio/tarteaucitron/consentmanager.net/Cookie Information/Klaro/Osano/CookieScript/Real Cookie Banner/CookieFirst/Cookiehub/Clickio/Secure Privacy), IAB consent strings (addtl_consent/usprivacy/GPP), CIS/Russia anti-bot WAFs (DDoS-Guard/Qrator/Variti), and Shopify telemetry. Fixed a consentmanager.net-vs-Quantcast prefix collision and a geo-specific FP where a non-English consent cookie containing 'token' was mis-flagged session_missing_httponly. 7 new table-driven tests with real German Borlabs/Complianz and French Axeptio/tarteaucitron banner fixtures; FP corpus gate unchanged (fp=0,fn=0). Verified live on real domains: lacompagniedescartes.fr 5 Shopify cookies other->tracker, dearchitect.nl didomi_interaction other->cmp, dreamjob.ru qrator_ssid2 mis-classed session->anti_bot. domain_cookies stores aggregate tallies not per-cookie names and has no tool_version column, so measurement was live-probe over geo-diverse real domains.

cors-tester.trl_evidence:
7-variant active prober (reflection, null, subdomain-suffix-bypass,
prefix-confusion, port-attack, case-fold, http-vs-https). Each variant
sends an OPTIONS preflight + GET, records the full ACAO/ACAC/Vary/
methods/headers surface, and feeds a documented severity matrix
(reflection x credentials, null x credentials, etc.). Worker pool of
4, per-probe 4s, total 30s. Composes with go-pentest-cors-misconfig-
prober via CORS_MISCONFIG_URL; gateable on bug-bounty scope via
SCOPE_GUARD_URL. Tests: probes_test (variant table, downgrade skip,
scheme preservation), evaluate_test (isReflection, isTrueACAC,
hasVaryOrigin, Classify matrix), handler_test (httptest servers for
reflective+credentials critical, wildcard-only low, suffix-bypass
critical, null-origin critical, safe server, case-fold, full JSON
shape) — 20+ test cases across 3 files. ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-
to-6.md captures the variant taxonomy and severity matrix.

crawl-web-application.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 — BFS crawler with worker pool (default 8), per-host rate limit
(golang.org/x/time/rate, 4 rps), robots.txt parsing via temoto/robotstxt
(RFC 9309), HTML parsing via golang.org/x/net/html (extracts <a>, <link
rel=canonical>, title; honors rel=nofollow), URL canonicalization +
cross-page dedup, SSRF-safe outbound via go-common/safehttp, scope

crlf-tester.trl_evidence:
Marker-based detection (8-byte per-scan unique hex marker) replaces
static-string echo checks; inspect() classifies header reflection
(critical), raw-newline-in-header (high), and body reflection (medium)
separately. Probes inject across query params, path segments, and four

Host gated by ALLOW_HOST_INJECT=1). Payload corpus is 20 variants
covering hex-case, double-encoding, Unicode/UTF-8 overlong, fragment-

(go-pentest-payloads). Per-request 4 s, total 60 s budget, worker pool
4. SCOPE_GUARD_URL fail-closed gate. ≥9 unit + integration tests
including httptest.Server vulnerable + safe backends.

crux-field-vitals.trl_evidence: Uses the official Chrome UX Report API to map real-user p75 and histogram data for LCP, CLS, INP, and TTFB, with explicit no-data/upstream-error handling, SSRF-safe egress, fixtures, and selftests.

css-framework-detector.description: Detects which CSS framework(s) a web page or raw HTML uses (Bootstrap, Tailwind, Bulma, Foundation, Materialize, Semantic/Fomantic UI, UIkit, Pure.css, Tachyons, Skeleton, Milligram) from three independent static-markup signals: linked-stylesheet / CDN hrefs (strong, often versioned), distinctive class-name signatures, and a generator meta hint. Returns frameworks[] (each name + confidence 0..1 + extracted version + evidence[]) sorted by confidence, the top framework, and a raw-signal summary.
css-framework-detector.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over golang.org/x/net/html (no regex over HTML structure): parses the whole document and recursively collects three independent signal families per candidate framework. (1) CDN / linked-stylesheet signal: every <link rel=stylesheet|preload href> and <script src> is matched case-insensitively against a curated needle table (bootstrap, tailwindcss/tailwind, bulma, foundation, materialize, fomantic/semantic-ui, uikit, purecss/pure-min, tachyons, skeleton, milligram) covering jsdelivr/cdnjs/unpkg/self-hosted filenames; this is the strong, author-declared signal and frequently carries an exact pinned version. (2) Class-name signatures: distinctive tokens (navbar, col-*, btn-*, d-flex; tailwind md:/lg:/sm:/pt-/px- prefixes + text-sm/bg-blue-500; bulma columns/is-*; foundation grid-x/callout; materialize waves-effect; uikit uk-*; pure-*; tachyons pa3/f6) are separated from generic shared tokens (container, row, flex, button, column, hero) that are flagged weak so a single one never yields a confident detection; Semantic UI's generic 'ui' token only counts as distinctive when it co-occurs with a real SUI partner (button/grid/menu/segment/...) on the same element. (3) <meta name=generator> naming a framework adds a soft corroborating hint. Version extraction: a single regexp pulls the dotted version from the common CDN shapes bootstrap@5.3.0, /bootstrap/5.3.0/, and bootstrap-5.3.0.min.css (stdlib regexp is used ONLY for class-token/CDN/version pattern matching, never to parse HTML structure). Confidence is a documented heuristic blend (scoreFramework): CDN hit base 0.80 plus 0.15 when a version was extracted; first distinctive class +0.25 with diminishing +0.10 each for the next two (class-only contribution capped at ~0.45 so class signatures alone never reach CDN-level certainty); weak classes +0.05 each capped at 0.10; generator +0.10; clamped to [0,1], 2dp; results below a 0.15 floor (e.g. a lone weak class) are dropped, and the list is sorted by confidence desc with a deterministic framework-order tie-break. Honest TRL-4 ceiling: this is a pure static-markup heuristic over a finite curated framework set; it cannot see browser-computed CSS, and minified / renamed / content-purged class names (e.g. a Tailwind build that purges unused utilities and inlines arbitrary values) can defeat the class-signature path entirely, leaving the CDN/link signal as the reliable one — frameworks outside the curated list are not guessed but reported as an empty frameworks[] with no_framework_detected in degraded[]. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so many producers scanning the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode (static DOM, no JS). SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) plus localhost/.local names, with cache-side and safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs; the input guard is OUTBOUND-only and never inspects hrefs/srcs embedded in the markup. Direct text= mode scans pasted HTML with no fetch. Per-failure notes pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed, empty_body, no_framework_detected) instead of failing the response; degraded[] is always a non-nil slice and frameworks[] is always non-nil. 36 unit + handler tests cover bootstrap by CDN and by classes, tailwind by utility classes and by CDN, bulma, foundation, materialize, semantic-ui by distinctive pair (and the bare-ui non-detection), uikit, pure.css, tachyons, skeleton + milligram by CDN, version extraction at @ / path / dash shapes and two-component versions, multiple frameworks on one page, confidence ordering and the class-below-CDN ceiling, the cap-at-one blend, generator meta hint, evidence de-duplication, raw-signal counts, single-weak-class rejection, protocol-relative CDN, garbage/empty input safety, plus handler text-mode, parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, and SSRF rejection. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (bootstrap CDN+version, tailwind classes, class-below-CDN ceiling, confidence ordering, no-framework, non-nil result, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

currency-detector.trl_evidence: Symbol regex + locale patterns + currency-switcher heuristics.

customer-logos.trl_ceiling_reason: CPU-only DOM heuristics plateau at principled multilingual structural extraction; exact brand disambiguation / image recognition (vs structural alt/anchor/grid signals) and SLA-grade live verdicts would need a browser engine or paid logo-recognition feed. No persisted logo-detail table exists to certify live accuracy against.
customer-logos.trl_evidence: Pass-3 geo de-biasing (locale.go): the extractor only knew US-English social-proof conventions (English path probes, English headings, English CSS class hints, English-only generic reject, Latin-only brand mangling). 1000-row 3.4.0 sample + live probes confirmed the gap: datev.de 404'd EVERY English path (/customers /about /case-studies /testimonials /enterprise) so its German reference wall was never fetched -> verdict=none; a fixture proved a 'Unsere Kunden' heading set HasHeading=false on every logo. v3.5.0 adds ~70 localized path probes (/kunden /referenzen /references /clientes /casos-de-exito /clienti /klanten /kunder /klienci /musteriler /導入事例 /고객사 /客户 …) deduped by resolved URL, ~130 localized social-proof headings across DE/FR/ES/PT/IT/NL/Nordic/PL/TR/RU+UA(Cyrillic)/JA/KO/ZH/VI/ID matched in lower-case and native script, localized grid-class hints (kundenlogos/logos-clients/clientes/jirei), localized generic-alt reject (Bild/imagen/ロゴ/客户/고객/иконка), and a script-aware normalizeBrand that skips Latin-only hash/dimension/vowel-ratio/titleCase heuristics for non-Latin brands (株式会社トヨタ/Лаборатория/삼성전자 preserved verbatim). Probe widened to Accept-Language '*' + 8 workers to fit the larger set in the 20s budget. Live before->after: factorial.es has ALL 5 English customer paths 404 and only Spanish /clientes + /nuestros-clientes 200 -> 3.5.0 fetches them and returns strong_social_proof (35 logos) where 3.4.0 saw the wall pages as nonexistent. Soft-404 SPA (doctolib.fr 200-on-all-paths) correctly held at weak_signal by the verdict ladder -- no new FP. 46 test funcs / 62 subtests incl. a 16-language heading-attribution matrix, an end-to-end German /kunden httptest, non-Latin brand preservation, and an English-regression guard; build/vet/test green, gofmt clean. CPU-only, CGO-free, no new deps, no runtime LLM, no new outbound calls.

dns-record.trl_evidence: v1.7.0 ACE/punycode-normalizes native Unicode domains via golang.org/x/net/idna (IDNA2008, BidiRule) before the DNS query; live IDN false-negative rate 18/18 -> 0/18 on real .рф/.укр/.рус/Korean/Greek/Arabic/Swiss domains; also widened MX gazetteer with regional hosts (Cloudflare Email Routing, Strato, ALL-INKL, IONOS regional TLDs, reg.ru/beget, naver, NetEase). New table-driven IDN tests across 7 scripts + e2e handler test.

dom-complexity-metrics.description: Structural DOM-complexity metrics for a web page or raw HTML — total element-node and text-node counts, maximum nesting depth, maximum sibling breadth, per-tag frequency histogram, distinct-tag count, average children per element, high-fan-out element count, the deepest element path, text-to-element ratio, total DOM size in bytes, and a combined complexity score.
dom-complexity-metrics.trl_evidence: Real single-pass DOM tree walk over golang.org/x/net/html's tokenizing parser (no regex over HTML structure): malformed markup is normalised into a well-formed tree, then one depth-first traversal in document order computes total element-node count, total text-node count, maximum element-nesting depth, maximum sibling breadth (widest direct-element-child fan at any parent), a per-lowercase-tag frequency histogram, distinct-tag count, average direct-element-children per element, count of elements exceeding the >60-child high-fan-out threshold (threshold echoed in the response), the deepest element path (root-to-deepest tag chain, first-in-document-order on ties), text-to-element ratio, total DOM size in bytes, and a documented combined complexity score (max_depth + max_breadth + element_nodes/100). The whole served document is counted including head/script/style — DOM weight is a property of the markup as a whole, distinct from prose extraction. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so 20 producers analysing the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode. SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode analyses pasted markup with no fetch. Per-failure notes pushed to a non-nil degraded[] (empty_document, fetch_failed, parse_failed) instead of failing the response. ~30 unit tests cover nested-div depth, N-sibling breadth, node counts, tag histogram, distinct-tag count, deepest-path chain (including first-wins-on-tie), high-fan-out threshold boundary (60 vs 61), text-node vs element-node counting, malformed-HTML normalisation, script/style inclusion, average-children and ratio math, complexity-score monotonicity, empty-document degraded, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, and text= mode. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (hand-built known depth/breadth, node counts, deepest path, fan-out, empty-document, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal. TRL-4 ceiling: metrics describe the STATIC server-rendered DOM only — no JS-expanded/hydrated DOM, no computed layout/CSSOM, no render-tree or paint cost; a headless-browser engine would be required to advance further.

domain-ad-network.description: Ad-network detector with validated ads.txt seller records and executable-context matching for 18 in-page advertising providers.
domain-ad-network.trl_evidence: v0.3.0 fixes a measured false-positive class: 213/221 cached ads_txt_present=true sample rows had zero valid seller lines. Presence now requires at least one syntactically valid seller declaration, rejecting HTTP-200 soft 404s, directive-only placeholders, and arbitrary comma-separated error text. In-page provider matching is restricted to executable script/iframe/ad-widget contexts, so prose, comments, policy links, and vendor lists no longer count as active ad-network evidence. Unit, race, vet, build, deterministic selftest, positive seller/provider, and negative soft-404/non-executable regressions are green.

domain-ai-bot-policy-scanner.trl_evidence: v0.3.0 is an RFC 9309 robots.txt parser (grouped User-agent stacks, Allow/Disallow longest-match precedence, * and $ wildcards) over a curated 40-token AI-crawler gazetteer with per-bot blocked/allowed classification, a per-signal evidence trail, plus /ai.txt, /.well-known/ai.txt, TDM-Reservation header and llmstxt.org-validated /llms.txt. Pass 3 measured the dominant FP on 1000 real v0.2.1 rows: 412/1000 (41.2%, incl. anthropic.com) listed the ENTIRE 40-bot gazetteer in allowed_ai_bots via the permissive User-agent:* fallback, falsely reading as a 40-crawler opt-IN; classifyBots now reports only NAME-SPECIFIC allows so allowed_ai_bots goes 412->0 leaked rows (live-verified: anthropic/children/christmas allowed 40->0, bigfootdiesel blocked=8 allowed 32->0). 23 table-driven tests; go build/vet/test green.

domain-amp-detector.description: AMP (Accelerated Mobile Pages) presence detector — link rel=amphtml + canonical AMP HTML.
domain-amp-detector.trl_evidence: Real HTML parser via golang.org/x/net/html (no regex); detects link rel=amphtml + canonical, <html amp> + <html ⚡> (U+26A1), AMP cache URL in og:url / twitter:url; case-insensitive attributes; SSRF-safe outbound via go-common/safehttp (10s timeout, <=3 redirects, 2 MiB body cap); verifies the linked AMP alternative by fetching it; per-failure errors pushed to degraded[]; 15 unit tests on canned HTML covering each signal; /selftest exercises both pure-logic checks and a live bbc.com probe.

domain-analytics-stack.trl_evidence: Real HTML parsing and a 28-tool international analytics catalogue combine static DOM, rendered DOM, cookies, headers, and network-log evidence. Per-tool IDs and source trails are surfaced; false-positive guards, graceful render degradation, six test files, and live selftests cover both positive and abstain paths.
domain-analytics-stack.trl_ceiling_reason: Consent-gated tags, WAF pages, and analytics loaded only after interaction remain outside a single homepage snapshot.

domain-anycast-footprint.description: Anycast vs unicast routing detector (multi-resolver IP convergence + ASN check).
domain-anycast-footprint.trl_evidence: Parallel A+AAAA UDP queries against 8 public resolvers (Cloudflare 1.1.1.1/1.0.0.1, Google 8.8.8.8, Quad9 9.9.9.9, OpenDNS 208.67.222.222, Verisign 64.6.64.6, Level3 4.2.2.2, DNS.WATCH 84.200.69.80) via miekg/dns; Team Cymru whois.cymru.com:43 bulk-mode ASN+org+prefix+country lookup; curated 17-entry anycast ASN allowlist; convergence + ASN allowlist decision tree (>=4-resolver convergence + allowlist match -> anycast/high); 5 handler tests with canned DNS+ASN fixtures (zero network); selftest probes pure-logic helpers + live cloudflare.com. Heuristic-grade, not RFC-grounded -> TRL 4.

domain-backlink-profile.description: Inbound-link / referring-domain estimator (Common Crawl + Web Archive).

domain-bimi-checker.description: BIMI logo record + VMC certificate checker (brand email indicators).
domain-bimi-checker.trl_evidence: BIMI parser (v=BIMI1, l=, a= tags), CNAME chain walk, safehttp HEAD-fetcher (https-only, SSRF-safe) for logo SVG + VMC cert, degraded[] semantics on per-source failure, 8 unit tests on canned TXT/HTTP, /selftest with live cnn.com DNS lookup. v0.1.0 first ship.

domain-breadcrumb-schema.description: schema.org BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + microdata detector + validator.

domain-cms-version.trl_evidence: Real DOM parsing across 18 CMS/SSG/headless families; strong-vs-weak evidence gate prevents CDN and hotlinked-asset false positives; direct static detection floor survives cache/render failures; 44+ unit and handler tests plus live selftest probes.
domain-cms-version.trl_ceiling_reason: Plugin/theme-level depth needs a Wappalyzer-grade signature database; bot-protected and fingerprint-less sites remain outside reliable CPU-only detection.

domain-compliance-claim-scanner.trl_evidence: Already real TRL-6 (banded classifier over visible text + evidence trail, 28 tests). Pass 3 removed a structural geographic bias: gazetteer 37->56 frameworks adding 19 multi-jurisdiction privacy regimes (LGPD/PIPL/PIPEDA/POPIA/APPI/PDPA/PIPA-KR/DPDP/KVKK/nFADP/Privacy-Act-AU/PDPL/LFPDPPP/UK-GDPR/Cyber-Essentials/IRAP/ISMAP/EN-301-549) matched in native script, GDPR aliases DSGVO/RGPD/AVG, multilingual claim verbs (de/fr/es/pt/nl/tr/ru + CJK 認証/準拠/遵守/认证/인증), ccTLD->jurisdiction inference, localized posture-page candidates, per-claim+aggregated jurisdiction output, and a boilerplate-hedge guard preserving Pass-2 precision. On 8 real non-US fixtures detection went 1/8->8/8; live, nubank.com.br now detects LGPD (was 0) and telekom.de detects GDPR via /datenschutz+DSGVO (was 0); stripe.com unchanged at 9 standards. 46 table-driven tests, build/vet/test green.
domain-compliance-claim-scanner.trl_ceiling_reason: Reaching 7 needs cross-checks against an external trust-registry / attestation feed (e.g. confirming a claimed SOC 2 against an auditor registry) — that requires a paid/external source, outside the CPU-only no-new-outbound-calls constraint.

domain-ct-log-watcher.trl_evidence: RFC4514 issuer-DN parsing, precert/leaf dedup, in/out-of-scope SAN split, CA-family/cost/ACME posture, source_health + degraded[]. Pass-3 adds RFC5891 IDNA2008 U-label<->A-label canonicalization so an IDN domain's own SANs (returned by crt.sh as Unicode) no longer mislabel out-of-scope vs a punycode apex; new unicode_sans display field. Representative IDN rows: 5/6 in-scope SANs wrongly out-of-scope before, 0 after. 25 table-driven tests (8 new IDN, Cyrillic/.de/.vet fixtures), go vet + gofmt clean.
domain-ct-log-watcher.trl_ceiling_reason: single free heavily rate-limited crt.sh upstream (429/502/504), no second CT source or paid feed, no persistence/cross-check

domain-dane-tlsa.description: DANE/TLSA record presence + cert binding validation (RFC 6698).

domain-deployment-fingerprint.description: Unified deployment fingerprint (PaaS + serverless + edge-compute detector).
domain-deployment-fingerprint.trl_evidence: One bounded probe combines homepage headers/HTML, API HEAD behavior, and DNS CNAME evidence across PaaS, serverless backend, edge compute, and static hosting. Claims require platform-pinning signals, preserve evidence and confidence, and are covered by directional fixtures, handler tests, and a live selftest.

domain-details.trl_evidence: RDAP-first via openrdap (IANA bootstrap, RFC 7483 structured fields incl. event actions, status codes, secureDNS); whois TCP-43 fallback via likexian/whois + whois-parser; per-source 5s / total 12s timeouts; 6h in-process cache; 8 unit tests over canned RDAP JSON + canned whois text fixtures (no network in CI); GDPR-aware contact handling; ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

domain-dnssec-validator.trl_evidence: Real DNSSEC chain validation: recomputes each parent DS digest from the apex DNSKEY (RFC 4034 5.1.4 / 4509) and verifies the apex DNSKEY RRSIG cryptographically with a validity-window check (RFC 4035 5.3, miekg/dns RRSIG.Verify) - not an AD-bit check. v0.3.0 adds IDNA2008 (RFC 5891) U-label to punycode A-label normalization before lookups: 7 real signed IDN zones (German/Russian/Swedish/CJK, verified in domainscope) that 0.2.1 mis-reported insecure/F when passed in Unicode form now correctly report signed/secure live. 18 table-driven tests incl. offline ECDSAP256-signed-zone fixture, IANA root KSK-2017 vector, and end-to-end IDN regression; race-clean; 83.9% coverage.
domain-dnssec-validator.trl_ceiling_reason: Single public validating resolver. Lifting to 8 needs multi-resolver quorum (a second independent validating resolver) and a full root-anchored walk (.->TLD->zone verifying RRSIGs at every cut). That is breadth, not the core crypto check, which is already real.

domain-documentation-platform-detector.trl_evidence: Static-first detection probes 13 documentation locations with 27 platform/asset/DOM fingerprints and a two-factor custom-docs gate; an opportunistic JS-render bonus can only add evidence, never erase the static floor. Includes false-positive guards, deterministic selection, provenance/degradation output, table-driven tests, and a live selftest.

domain-ecommerce-platform.trl_evidence: Real DOM parsing and a volume-immune, multi-kind evidence gate cover 23 global and regional commerce platforms. CDN-only references abstain while backend/runtime signals publish; a direct static floor survives cache failure and optional JS rendering only adds evidence. Directional fixtures, handler tests, retry/error taxonomy, and live selftest are present.

domain-email-deliverability-score.trl_evidence: RFC-compliant parsers for 6 email-auth record families (SPF 7208 lookup-limit PermError, DMARC 7489 policy ladder, DKIM 6376 key parse + catch-all guard, MX 5321/7505 null-MX, BIMI VMC) with a per-decision evidence trail and weighted 0-100 score. Pass-3 adds IDNA2008 U-label to A-label punycode normalization (golang.org/x/net/idna) before DNS lookups: 12/12 Unicode IDN mail domains (Japanese/Korean/Cyrillic/Latin-diacritic/Hebrew/Catalan) went from 0/F false positive to real scores 47-61 on live 0.3.0. 14 IDN punycode-vector tests + 1 handler IDN test added; full suite green, CGO-free.
domain-email-deliverability-score.trl_ceiling_reason: No live SMTP-delivery / inbox-placement / sending-reputation feed; DKIM selector discovery is gazetteer-bounded, not exhaustive — the production-grade deliverability signals require paid feeds.

domain-error-monitoring.description: Front-end error monitoring detector - Sentry/Datadog RUM/Bugsnag/Rollbar/Raygun/etc.
domain-error-monitoring.trl_evidence: Real HTML parsing plus W3C Reporting API, Report-To, Reporting-Endpoints, and NEL response-header detection covers 22 error-monitoring SDK and browser-reporting patterns. Evidence positions, identifiers, degradation output, directional tests, and live selftests are present.
domain-error-monitoring.trl_ceiling_reason: First-party bundled SDKs with no static or response-header fingerprint require reliable post-hydration browser execution.

domain-experimentation-stack.trl_evidence: Real HTML parsing with evidence-position trails covers 19 A/B-testing and feature-flag tools across script, inline, meta, iframe, and cookie signals. Static-first fetching escalates to shared JS rendering without losing the static result; 28+ directional tests, SSRF guards, stable ordering, and live selftest evidence are present.
domain-experimentation-stack.trl_ceiling_reason: Server-side experimentation can emit no client-observable fingerprint; measured JS rendering recovered almost no additional vendor signal and remains a flaky upstream.

domain-faq-schema.description: schema.org FAQPage JSON-LD + microdata detector + validator.

domain-favicon-completeness.description: Favicon + Web App Manifest icon-set completeness checker.

domain-font-stack.description: Web font stack detector - Google Fonts/Adobe Fonts/self-hosted + family count + font-display hygiene.

domain-glue-records.description: Glue record presence at parent zone (RFC 1034 3.6.1 / RFC 8499).

domain-http3-quic-detector.trl_evidence: 0.4.0 IDNA2008-encodes (golang.org/x/net/idna strict Lookup profile) every host to its A-label before the TLS/QUIC/ALPN dial; previously a raw Unicode IDN was handed to crypto/tls ServerName + net dial which reject non-ASCII, failing 'no such host' = a false 'no HTTP/3' on every IDN. Over 40 real registered IDNs (PT/CZ/ZH/TR/RU scripts), old code produced a broken non-ASCII dial target 40/40; new code correctly A-label-encodes 40/40. Atop the existing RFC 7838 Alt-Svc parser + real quic-go UDP/443 handshake + TCP/443 ALPN probe + evidence trail. 40 table-driven unit cases incl. 9 multi-script IDN + bidi/noncharacter rejections; build/test/vet green.
domain-http3-quic-detector.trl_ceiling_reason: Single CPU-only probe; true TRL 8 needs independent QUIC cross-checks / IP-path diversity / persistence, out of scope for one detector.

domain-integration-marketplace.description: Integration / marketplace / app-directory page detector with count estimate.
domain-integration-marketplace.trl_evidence: Static-first marketplace detection combines JSON-LD ItemList, integration-link grids, repeated logo cards, and list-class signals with deduplication and false-positive guards. Shared JS rendering fits the 65-second budget and can only upgrade the static result; provenance is surfaced and roughly 26 table-driven tests plus live checks are green.

domain-mobile-app-resolver.trl_evidence: 0.4.0 adds regional/alternative app-store parsing (Huawei AppGallery, Samsung Galaxy Store, Xiaomi GetApps, Amazon Appstore guarded against bare product pages) into a new regional_apps[] field + evidence trail with presence flip and findings, plus a ccTLD-aware iTunes storefront (storefrontForDomain: 48 ccTLDs, .uk/.co.uk->gb, gTLD->us) replacing the hardcoded country=us with no new outbound call. Store-type coverage 2/6 -> 6/6; localized Apple /de/ and Play hl=/gl= URLs confirmed already locale-agnostic. 6 new table-driven geo test funcs (22 total) with real regional + multilingual fixtures; offline regional-store /selftest exercised live by the deploy smoke gate; go vet/gofmt clean. Live /version==0.4.0, stripe/instagram/chihlee/dominte re-probed with no regression.
domain-mobile-app-resolver.trl_ceiling_reason: Reaching TRL 8 needs a paid/queryable cross-store feed (or JS-rendered fetch tier) to confirm an app truly exists in each regional store rather than parsing the advertised link; outside the CPU-only / no-paid-feed / no-new-outbound-call envelope.

domain-newsletter-platform-detector.trl_evidence: Multi-signal ESP fingerprinting covers 43 international, enterprise, publisher, SaaS, and self-hosted vendors using CDN/script hosts, form actions, hidden fields, widgets, and signup endpoints. Confidence gating separates generic subscribe forms from vendor attribution; evidence and abstentions are explicit, with 33+ directional tests and two-tier rendered/static fetching.

domain-open-source-presence.trl_evidence: Evidence-trail organization attribution uses brand correlation plus GitHub API repository verification, eliminating generic footer-icon false positives. It has explicit unreachable/no-data taxonomy, bounded retry, directional tests, and a live selftest false-positive guard.

domain-passive-dns-history.trl_evidence: Already-real passive-DNS history service (canonical IANA RRTYPE normalization, IP re-serialization, RFC 1035/1123 hostname validation, RFC 7505 null-MX preservation, window-unioning dedup with per-record source evidence trail, family classification, feed-free /24+/48 network-prefix churn metric). Pass 3 closed a geographic / non-Latin-script FALSE NEGATIVE: every source and the domainscope catalog key on the ASCII A-label (punycode) form, so a Unicode-form query (münchen.de, 日本語.jp, почта.рф) was sent verbatim and could NEVER match -> every non-Western IDN returned 'no history'. normalizeDomain now runs the IDNA2008 Lookup profile (golang.org/x/net/idna, pure-Go/CGO-free, non-transitional UTS-46 + BidiRule) before any source/catalog query and decodes the A-label back to a unicode_domain evidence breadcrumb; canonHostname/looksLikeHostname IDNA-encode Unicode CNAME/NS/MX rdata so a U-label target and its punycode spelling dedupe to ONE record instead of the Unicode one being silently dropped by the old ASCII-only gate. 36 table-driven unit tests (7 IDN-specific, real verified punycode fixtures DE/JP/CN/KR/RU/FR). Live-proven on v0.3.0: münchen.de and xn--mnchen-3ya.de both return domain=xn--mnchen-3ya.de unicode_domain=münchen.de; почта.рф returns domain=xn--80a1acny.xn--p1ai unicode_domain=почта.рф. go build/vet/test green, gofmt clean.
domain-passive-dns-history.trl_ceiling_reason: Comprehensive multi-year passive-DNS history requires a paid feed (Farsight DNSDB / SecurityTrails); the free public sources are sparse + rate-limited (HackerTarget hostsearch/dnslookup) and the formerly-primary Mnemonic public read API is decommissioned (CloudFront 502). Cannot break the ceiling CPU-only without a new paid outbound source, which the rules forbid.

domain-podcast-presence.description: Podcast presence detector — RSS feed + linked platforms + embedded players.

domain-product-count.description: E-commerce catalog lower-bound estimator with canonicalized sitemap, JSON-LD, Shopify, and homepage product signals.

Positive distinct-product cases and negative duplicate/placeholder cases pass unit, race, vet, build, and deterministic selftest coverage.

domain-rbl-reputation.trl_evidence: v0.3.0 RFC-compliant DNSBL return-code decoder (Spamhaus SBL/CSS/XBL/PBL+DBL, SpamCop, Barracuda, SORBS, URIBL/SURBL bitmask), error-sentinel detection (127.255.255.x open-resolver, URIBL/SORBS 127.0.0.1 refused), TXT listing-reason fetch, per-list evidence trail, resolution-aware grade (U for unresolved), coverage+confidence model. Pass 3 adds IDNA2008 U-label->A-label normalization (golang.org/x/net/idna, CGO-free transitive dep) before any DNS/DNSBL query: live before, mueller.de/cafe.fr/rossiya.rf/zhongguo.cn/barid.eg all returned resolution=unresolved grade=U over 2 zones (a geo false-negative under the container's pure resolver); after, each is queried as its xn-- A-label - the 3 hosted ones (DE/FR/RU) flip to resolved grade A over 8 zones, the 2 unhosted (CN/EG) correctly return an honest U on the right A-label name (verified no A record via 1.1.1.1). 86 table-driven unit tests across 11 scripts.
domain-rbl-reputation.trl_ceiling_reason: DNSBL verdicts are bounded by free public blocklist coverage and the shared resolver vantage point; SLA-grade reputation (TRL 8-9) needs paid/commercial threat feeds, multiple resolver vantage points, and historical listing persistence that this CPU-only DNS-query service cannot provide.

domain-saas-host-mapper.trl_evidence: Table-driven CNAME/MX/NS/A + server/x-powered-by/body fingerprint gazetteer with per-match evidence trail; CDN/email/DNS signals never elected as host. Pass 3 fixed the single biggest FP in the table (~145k self-hosted WordPress sites mis-elected as the wordpress_com SaaS via an over-broad wp.com body token; 99.96% of stored wordpress_com rows had no *.wordpress.com CNAME) by adding a cms category that never wins the host slot, added 20 international builders (B12, Odoo/BE, JouwWeb+Webador/NL, Docplanner/PL, DonDominio/ES, SITE123/IL, Strikingly, Portfoliobox/SE, Readymag, Pixieset, Shopline/APAC, Tistory/KR, BigCartel, Turbify, Hibu, Homestead, Thryv, MoxiWorks, wpenginepowered) + 5 long-tail CDNs incl. two Chinese edges, and added IDNA2008 U-label->A-label normalization. Live re-measure: 14/16 geo under-detection domains now land a host (was 0), 4/4 WordPress FPs corrected, 3/3 IDN domains now resolve as A-labels. 21 table-driven unit tests, CPU-only, CGO-free.
domain-saas-host-mapper.trl_ceiling_reason: Fingerprint-only (DNS+HTTP-header) detection; an origin behind a generic CDN/anycast front (Cloudflare, AWS Global Accelerator) is structurally undeterminable without a browser engine or paid host-intel feed.

domain-structured-data-rollup.trl_evidence: v0.3.0 adds case-insensitive @type canonicalisation (canonical.go) so real CMS markup with lower/mixed-case types (organization, localbusiness, person, store, restaurant, PRODUCT, @id-fragment #localbusiness) folds to schema.org canonical casing before hierarchy/hint/validation lookup — 265 distinct corpus domains lost their hierarchy+business hint pre-fix; plus new country-agnostic locales[] (BCP-47 e.g. de-DE/pt-BR/ja-JP/zh-TW) + languages[] (ISO-639) fields from og:locale/og:locale:alternate/<html lang>/JSON-LD inLanguage, and IDNA2008 host punycode normalisation (idn.go). Live before->after confirmed: everydayfields.com business_hints [publisher]->[organization,publisher]; ezgenloc.com gained locale zh-TW + organization hint; djnichenov.com gained locale ru. 9 new table-driven tests; build/vet/gofmt green.

domain-support-stack-detector.trl_evidence: Calibrated multi-signal support-stack detection separates high-confidence loader hosts, medium bootstrap symbols, and low brand/status links. A single weak footer or prose signal can never assert installation; corroboration, evidence trails, vendor-level confidence, false-positive regression fixtures, and live production validation cover the supported catalogue.

domain-tech-eol-flagger.trl_evidence: Offline EOL gazetteer (nginx/Apache/IIS/OpenSSL/PHP/Python/Node/JDK/.NET/Ubuntu/Debian/CentOS/MySQL/MariaDB/PostgreSQL/Tomcat/jQuery/Bootstrap/AngularJS/WordPress/Drupal/Joomla/TYPO3/Elementor) with cycle-key resolution + dated classifier + per-tech evidence trail. Pass-3 0.3.0 closed three real-data gaps: IDNA2008 host punycode normalisation (x/net/idna) so Unicode hosts fetch instead of being percent-encoded into an unresolvable authority; dropped the Bootstrap ?ver= regex that misread a WordPress asset version (bootstrap.js?ver=6.8.5 -> bogus Bootstrap 6.8.5, live on gogi.in); extended nginx to odd mainline+pre-1.10 (0.5-1.27) and PHP to pre-5.3 (4.3-5.2) so the oldest banners on non-Western budget hosting flag eol instead of unknown (nginx 1.21.3 child-adolesc.jp/.jp, 1.6.2 pizzaskazka.ru/.ru, 1.4.6 igate.com.ua/.ua all corrected live). 4 actionable FP/FN on inspected real domains -> 0. 17 test funcs / 91 cases, build+vet+gofmt green.

domain-third-party-fetch-map.trl_evidence: PSL-aware (publicsuffix) + IDNA2008-lookup (idna) registrable-domain resolution so Unicode and punycode forms of a host collapse to one party; ~370-entry gazetteer over 17 categories incl. new geo-diverse 'reviews' (Enamad/Samandehi Iran, Sitest Japan, Trusted Shops/eKomi Germany, Avis Verifies France, Trustpilot/Judge.me/Yotpo); per-origin evidence trail + tracker-density score. Pass-3 dropped the dominant over-counting FP (rel=profile/pingback/EditURI XFN namespace, 116/1000 rows) and reclassified 275 mis-bucketed third parties; third-party 'other' origins 708->317 (55.2% reduction) on the 1000-row corpus. 14 table-driven test functions.
domain-third-party-fetch-map.trl_ceiling_reason: Static HTML parse only sees markup-declared resources; capturing runtime XHR/fetch()/dynamically-injected third parties (TRL 8+) needs a real headless browser, which is structurally out of scope for a CPU-only Go service.

embed-iframe-classifier.description: Inventories and classifies every <iframe>, <embed>, and <object> on a web page or raw HTML: resolved-absolute src/data, host, and a curated provider/category label (youtube/vimeo video, google_maps/openstreetmap maps, twitter/facebook social, spotify/soundcloud audio, google_docs/codepen docs, typeform/airtable forms, ads_analytics), plus per-provider and per-category counts.
embed-iframe-classifier.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over golang.org/x/net/html (no regex over HTML structure): parses the whole document and recursively collects every <iframe>/<embed>/<object> in document order, descending into <object> subtrees so a fallback iframe/embed nested as object content is still inventoried. The source attribute is picked per element (src for iframe/embed, data for object) and resolved to an absolute URL against the page's final post-redirect fetched URL via net/url ResolveReference, so a relative or protocol-relative src is reported absolute with a derived lower-cased host. Classification is by registrable-domain host SUFFIX (host == suffix or ends with .suffix) so www./m./player./open. subdomains all land on the same provider, and a couple of path checks disambiguate same-host providers (google.com/maps -> google_maps; docs.google.com/forms -> google_forms ahead of generic google_docs). The curated provider table covers video (youtube incl. youtube-nocookie/youtu.be, vimeo, dailymotion, wistia, loom, twitch), maps (google_maps, openstreetmap), social (twitter incl. x.com, facebook incl. fb/fb.watch/facebook.net, instagram, linkedin, reddit, tiktok), audio (spotify, soundcloud, anchor), docs (google_docs, slideshare, scribd, codepen, jsfiddle, codesandbox, github_gist), forms (typeform, airtable, google_forms), and ads_analytics (doubleclick, googlesyndication, googletagmanager, google-analytics). Anything not on the finite list falls to provider/category other rather than being guessed — the honest TRL-4 ceiling, since novel embed hosts cannot be classified without an external dataset (the TRL-5+ ceiling). A src-less iframe (srcdoc-only / JS-filled) is still counted with provider/category inline and an empty host. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so many producers scanning the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode (static DOM, no JS). SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs; the input guard is OUTBOUND-only and never inspects relative iframe srcs. Direct text= mode scans pasted HTML with no fetch (empty base, so relative srcs stay as-is). Per-failure notes pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed, empty_body, no_embeds, srcless_embed) instead of failing the response; degraded[] is always a non-nil slice. 30 unit tests cover every provider family, category bucketing, host-suffix subdomain matching, lookalike-host rejection, embed src vs object data, relative + protocol-relative resolution, unresolved-without-base, srcless inline handling, by_provider/by_category counts, document-order preservation, embeds anywhere in the tree, object-nested fallback, no-embeds, garbage/data-URI input safety, plus handler text-mode, parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, and SSRF rejection. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (youtube/vimeo classification, relative resolution, object data, srcless inline, host-suffix subdomains, by_provider/by_category counts, unknown->other, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

emotional-tone.trl_ceiling_reason: CPU-only lexicon/cue method: no sarcasm/irony/syntactic context, and per-language cue lists are necessarily positive-skewed; reaching 6 needs RFC-grade evidence trails + production cross-checks beyond a lexicon.
emotional-tone.trl_evidence: Pass-3 geo: added CPU-only page-language detection (Unicode script census for CJK/Hangul/Cyrillic/Greek/Arabic/Hebrew/Thai/Devanagari + a function-word stopword vote for en/es/fr/de/pt/it/nl + diacritic signal) and per-language routing. Non-English text now scores against hand-curated polarity cue lists (es/fr/de/pt/it/nl + basic ja/zh/ko/ru, no English false friends) or is gated to neutral with language_supported=false. On 101 live non-English ccTLD domains the cross-language false-friend/sparse-English false-positive class dropped from 69/101 to ~0 (all 67 post-fix non-neutral labels backed by a same-language cue, 0 unbacked); negative misfires 30->7; 94/100 pages now scored in their own language vs 6 before. Output gained detected_language/language_confidence/language_script/language_supported/score_engine. 37 Go tests; go build/vet/test green, gofmt clean.

fleet-backup.description: F6 fleet backup orchestrator. Every 6h snapshots configured sources (SQLite, git, files, directories), encrypts with age (multiple recipients for key rotation), pushes to URI-shaped destination (initial: local:///opt/backups; future: storage-box:// rsync over SSH). Verifies every write by decrypt+tar-diff round-trip; mismatch = FAILED run, retention prune skipped. Per-instance SQLite audit log enforces "never delete what we didn't create" invariant. 7-daily / 4-weekly / 12-monthly retention. POST /restore always dry-run; actual restore is operator-driven with offline age identity (daemon never holds the decrypting key).
fleet-backup.trl_ceiling_reason: Verify-after-write in production is byte-equality on the encrypted blob (daemon holds no decrypting identity, by design). Full encrypt+decrypt+tar-diff verify only runs in tests + when an operator opts a verifying identity into the daemon. Lift to 7+ would either issue the daemon a dedicated verify-only identity or require every restore retrieval to verify before exposing bytes.
fleet-backup.trl_evidence: Initial scaffold (2026-05-19). 19 unit tests with injectable TarFn/EncryptFn/Destination + :memory: audit DB — tests never touch real disk or run heavy crypto on the hot path. Covered: AGE_RECIPIENTS empty/malformed/blank-only refuses to start (fail-CLOSED on missing crypto); malformed-key error redacts key body (no leak to logs); encrypt+decrypt + tar+untar round-trip; recipient fingerprint never contains raw pubkey; classify retention against 365 daily inputs / sparse inputs / multiple-same-day / zero-bands / non-RFC3339-rejected / deterministic-against-synthetic-clock; orchestrator successful snapshot writes audit row with recipient FP; destination-unreachable writes no blobs AND no audit rows; verify-mismatch marks run FAILED AND skips retention prune (safety invariant: failed run = no safe to delete history); retention-prune respects unowned-snapshot invariant (manually-placed blobs survive); /status JSON shape carries last_run/next_run/runs/audit/recipient_fingerprints/destination_ready; /restore defaults dry-run; /restore dry_run=false rejected without confirm_restore=YES; /run admin-gated (403 without token); /selftest 200 happy path + 503 on destination unreachable; parseSources rejects malformed (missing kind, unknown kind, missing path, duplicate names); LocalDest round-trip on real tmpdir; storage-box:// NotImplemented surfaces cleanly.

fleet-host-guard.description: F7 disk pressure eviction + F9 memory leak detector. Dockerhost guardian daemon: every 5 min samples fs usage and runs `docker image prune --filter until=168h` + truncates oversized non-rotated /var/log/*.log; every 1 h samples container RSS via `docker stats` and fits a linear regression — flags containers whose slope > 50 MB/h AND R² > 0.7.
fleet-host-guard.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by docker-stats RSS fidelity (cgroup memory.usage_in_bytes includes page cache). Lift to 7+ would require parsing /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/{slice}/memory.stat to separate RSS from cache.
fleet-host-guard.trl_evidence: Initial scaffold (2026-05-19). 11 unit tests across both primitives — injectable Runner + StatFS mocks; tests cover disk sweep below/above threshold (no-op / dry-run / real exec+parse), statfs failure path, log truncate keep-tail correctness, logrotate sibling skip, parseHumanBytes coverage, monotonic growth flagged (slope ~100MB/h, R² > 0.99), flat noise NOT flagged (R² < 0.7), noisy growth low R², leaky vs steady end-to-end ticker, docker-sock unreachable (state untouched, error surfaced). /selftest exercises regression + parsing primitives against in-memory data.

fleet-pipe.description: Ephemeral, unauthenticated cross-machine copy/paste relay. POST/PUT a blob with `curl --data-binary @-`, get back a URL, pull it on another machine within a hard ≤5-minute TTL. In-RAM only (restart wipes everything), 10 MiB/blob with a total-RAM budget. Random base62 ids, write-once or last-write-wins (?replace) named slots, say-it-out-loud ids (?words), ?burn one-time reads, ?qr scannable links, ?notify read-receipts. Optional client-side E2E: `-e` (AES-256-CTR+HMAC, key in the URL #fragment — opens in a browser or decrypts with openssl) plus a browser ask-me-a-secret sealed-box (ECDH P-256). `curl …/install | sh` adds a `pipe` client with one-time wormhole codes and an end-to-end universal clipboard. Browser app at GET / for non-CLI users.
fleet-pipe.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node, in-RAM, stateless relay by design — no persistence, no HA/clustering, no cross-checks. Deliberately minimal; further TRL would require features that contradict the ephemeral intent.
fleet-pipe.trl_evidence: v0.4.1 in-RAM TTL relay (PutOpts API, total-RAM byte budget, last-write-wins ?replace slots, read-receipt watch channels). Store unit tests: put/get, burn-on-read, TTL expiry via injected clock, write-once, replace, expired-name reclaim, max-entries + max-total-bytes budgets, byte accounting across burn/expire, clamp-TTL, base62 id shape/uniqueness over 1000 draws, PutRandom uniqueness over 500, 256-word list uniqueness, word-code shape. Handler/HTTP tests: random+named push/pull, write-once 409, replace overwrite, reserved/invalid name, burn-once-then-404, content negotiation (HTML app vs text vs ?shell/?app/?raw), word-code create, QR text+png, read-receipt notify+watch token/gone, safe headers, delete, 413. /selftest runs put→get-multi→burn→named→reserved→empty→ttl→replace→word-code→byte-budget end-to-end. Cross-impl crypto proven: AES-256-CTR+HMAC openssl↔WebCrypto round-trips (incl. 256 KB + macOS LibreSSL) and ECDH P-256 sealed-box round-trip + wrong-key rejection (node WebCrypto). Live real-browser verification (2026-06-01): terminal-encrypt→browser-decrypt, browser-compose→CLI-decrypt, and ask-me-a-secret reply sealed→inbox decrypt with the server holding ciphertext only. crypto/rand ids, rejection sampling (no modulo bias).

founding-year.trl_ceiling_reason: Live verdict on bot-hostile mega-brands (bmw.de/leroymerlin.fr/decathlon.fr/mango.com) is bounded by the upstream fetch tier: their about/company pages return empty bodies (JS-heavy/bot-blocked), so non-English founding text never reaches the parser and the verdict falls back to whois. There is no domainscope/stored-output table to measure live accuracy against. TRL 8 (SLA-grade live extraction) is not provable without a warmer fetch/render tier or a persisted output table; the extractor logic itself is sound and fully unit-tested.
founding-year.trl_evidence: Pass-3 geo: locale-aware founding-year extraction (intl.go). Latin-script founding verbs in DE/FR/ES/PT/IT/NL/PL/RU (gegruendet/fonde/fundada/fondata/opgericht/zalozona/osnovana) matched diacritic-insensitively, plus weak seit/depuis/desde/dal/sinds (copyright-tier, capped 0.45). CJK markers with no word boundaries: JP setsuritsu/sougyou/souritsu+nen, CN chengli-yu/chuangli-yu, KR seollip/changlip. European DD.MM.YYYY year extraction without tripping the version/digit-run guard. FP guards: native relative phrasing (20 Jahre/30 ans), native copyright footers, non-USD euro prices, JP phone digit-runs all produce NO founding candidate. Native explicit verbs are anchor-tier (beat whois/wayback in reconciler+inference). Fixture founding-phrase extraction went 0/16 -> 16/16 across 15 languages/3 scripts. Live-confirmed firing on fetchable non-English sites: gulbenkian.pt -> 1956 (matched_text 'es: desde 1956', correct), orf.at -> 'de: seit 2008', azzurro.it -> 'it: Dal 1987'. 67 table-driven tests (race-clean) incl. 7 new multilingual/multi-script suites with FP guards; build/vet/test/gofmt green. Pure Go, CGO-free, no new deps, no runtime LLM, no new outbound calls.

Detector logic is sound and fully unit-tested; TRL 8 (SLA-grade live) is not provable without a warmer fetch tier or a persisted output table.

Added geoRoundRegex + geoFundingVerbRegex (FR/DE/ES/PT-BR/IT/NL/SE/PL/RU/TR/AR/CN/JP/KR/ID), cjkAmountRegex+normalizeCJKAmount (5000万美元->$50M, 10億円->¥1B, 100억원->₩10B), suffixAmountRegex+normalizeSuffixAmount (25 millones de euros->€25M, 500 млн рублей->₽500M), and 9+ new currencies (R$/₩/₺/₽/zł/kr/CHF/MXN/Rs). 5 new table-driven tests; build/vet/test/gofmt green. TRL 6 (real). Beyond endpoint discovery + introspection summary,

See docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

heading-outline-extractor.description: Extracts the heading structure (h1-h6) of a web page or raw HTML and builds a nested document outline plus accessibility diagnostics — per-level counts, max nesting depth, and structure warnings (no_h1, multiple_h1, first_heading_not_h1, skipped_level, empty_heading).
heading-outline-extractor.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over the golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer (no regex for HTML structure): recurses the parsed document in document order collecting every h1-h6 with its resolved numeric level, collapsed visible text, and id attribute. skipTags subtrees (script/style/noscript/svg/template/head/iframe/object/embed) are pruned before text collection so an h-tag embedded in an inline SVG title or a <template> never pollutes the outline, and script/style text never leaks into a heading's label. The nested outline is built with a single-pass ancestor-stack algorithm (pop until the stack top is a strictly-lower level, attach, push) which is the standard 'nest under the most recent heading of a lower level' rule — it tolerates skipped levels (h1 directly to h3 still nests the h3 under the h1), multiple roots (each h1 starts a new root), and a leading deep heading without losing any node. Reports per-level counts (h1..h6 + total), max nesting depth, and an advisory issues[] of accessibility/structure warnings (no_h1, multiple_h1, first_heading_not_h1, skipped_level:hX->hY for any deeper-by-more-than-one jump, empty_heading) — all advisory, never fatal. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) so N producers analysing the same URL trigger one upstream fetch; plain-HTML render mode (heading structure lives in the served HTML, not a JS-rendered DOM — the documented TRL-4 ceiling for SPAs that build headings client-side). Direct text= mode analyses pasted HTML with no fetch. SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Per-failure notes pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed, no_extractable_text, no_headings) instead of failing the response; a page with zero headings still returns 200. 26 unit tests cover document-order extraction, id capture, whitespace collapse, script/style stripping inside headings, inline-markup flattening, template/svg pruning, outline nesting (simple/deep/skipped-level/multiple-roots/leading-deep), depth measurement, per-level counts, every diagnostic (no_h1, multiple_h1, skipped_level, empty_heading, ascent-is-not-a-skip, clean-document), parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, and the handler text-mode + no_headings degraded paths. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (order, skipped-level, multiple-h1, empty-heading, nested depth, script/style stripping, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.

hsts-checker.trl_ceiling_reason: needs real browser DOM for verification
hsts-checker.trl_evidence:
Pure-Go RFC 6797 directive parser with case-insensitive directive names,
quoted-string handling, negative/garbage rejection, and whitespace
tolerance (parse_test.go covers 9 cases). Embedded Chromium HSTS preload
list snapshot revision 2026-05-16.1 (~190 entries) with TLD-wide
treatment for .gov/.dev/.app/.page/.new and parent-domain inheritance.
Plaintext HTTP→HTTPS redirect probe captures the full chain (up to 5
hops) and reports the final scheme. Standalone TLS handshake check
records negotiated version, cipher suite, full chain length, and
performs explicit hostname + chain verification against the system
trust store. Grading rubric (F/D/C/B/A/A+) per
docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md, with A+ requiring corroboration from
the bundled snapshot (not just the preload directive on the wire).
Test surface: parse_test.go + preload_test.go + grade_test.go +
handler_test.go (httptest.NewTLSServer for HSTS variants, separate
http test server for the redirect probe).

ipv6-checker.trl_evidence:
v2.0.0 uplift from TRL 3 to TRL 6:
- Per-record A/AAAA resolution via miekg/dns with explicit TTL +
  source-resolver attribution (1.1.1.1 / 8.8.8.8 fallback).

  classification matrix + probe + happy-eyeballs unit tests pass.
- 15s total request budget enforced via context.WithTimeout.

js-framework-version-extractor.description: Detects JavaScript frameworks/libraries and their versions from STATIC markup only (no JS execution) on a web page or raw HTML: parses versioned CDN <script src> URLs (react@18.2.0, vue@3.4.21, jquery-3.7.1.min.js, bootstrap, htmx.org, alpinejs, gsap, d3, lodash, three), the exact ng-version attribute (gold case), build-tool/framework fingerprints (Next.js /_next/, Nuxt __NUXT__/_nuxt/, Gatsby /page-data/ + ___gatsby, SvelteKit /_app/, Astro astro-island, React data-reactroot, Vue data-v-*), and <meta name=generator> (Gatsby/WordPress/Hugo). Returns frameworks[] (name, version omitempty, confidence 0..1, evidence[]) sorted by confidence desc, plus top.
js-framework-version-extractor.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over golang.org/x/net/html (no regex over HTML structure): parses the whole document and recursively inspects every element for framework signals, accumulating one entry per framework name merged across signals. The gold case is the ng-version attribute carried on a rendered Angular root node, which embeds the EXACT runtime version (e.g. ng-version="17.1.0") and is scored highest (0.97). Versioned <script src> CDN URLs are the strong case (0.90): a curated regexp table pulls the version out of jsdelivr/unpkg @name@version paths, cdnjs /name/version/ layouts, and classic filename versions (jquery-3.7.1.min.js, bootstrap.bundle-5.3.2.min.js) for react (incl. react-dom -> react), vue, angularjs 1.x, jquery, bootstrap, htmx (htmx.org@1.9.10), alpinejs, gsap, d3, lodash, three; major-only pins (vue@3, d3@7, htmx.org@1.9) and protocol-relative srcs parse correctly. <meta name=generator> contributes a product + optional version (0.85) for Gatsby/Next/Nuxt/Hugo/WordPress/Astro/Svelte/VuePress/Jekyll/Docusaurus. Structural build-tool fingerprints (0.45-0.60) detect Next.js (id=__NEXT_DATA__, /_next/static/), Nuxt (window.__NUXT__ inline state, /_nuxt/), Gatsby (/page-data/, id=___gatsby), SvelteKit (/_app/), Astro (<astro-island>), React (data-reactroot/data-reactid), and Vue (data-v-* scoped-style attrs, data-server-rendered) WITHOUT a version. frameworks[] is sorted by confidence desc then name, every entry carries an evidence[] trail explaining why it fired, and version is omitted when unresolved. The honest TRL-4 ceiling: static markup only — a framework loaded via a dynamic import or bundled+minified with no version token in the URL is detected by fingerprint at best, frequently without a version; this does NOT execute JS or inspect the runtime (window.React.version), and an unversioned generic bundle is reported as nothing rather than guessed. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) in plain-HTML render mode (static DOM). SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) + localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs; the guard is OUTBOUND-only. Per-failure notes pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed, empty_body, no_framework_detected, version_unresolved) instead of failing the response; degraded[] is always a non-nil slice. 40+ unit tests cover every CDN library + version-parsing edge case (.min.js, .slim, .bundle, major-only @N, cdnjs /lib/ver/ layout, protocol-relative, react-dom->react, react-router NOT->react), ng-version exact + confidence ordering vs fingerprints, every structural fingerprint, generator meta with/without version, multiple frameworks, fingerprint-without-version omitting version, unversioned-bundle-not-guessed, no-framework empty, garbage input safety, plus handler text-mode, parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, and the degraded[] notes. /selftest exercises the pure-logic pipeline (react CDN version, ng-version exact, jquery filename version, Next.js fingerprint without version, generator meta, confidence ordering, no-framework empty, SSRF guard) AND a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal.
