team-member-extractor.description: Extracts a roster of team / staff members from an about-us or team page (or raw HTML) — each member's name, role (job title), short bio, photo_url (resolved absolute), and social/profile links — preferring schema.org Person (JSON-LD then microdata) and falling back to a conservative repeated-card heuristic. Returns members[] plus count and the detection method.
team-member-extractor.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over a fetched HTML page (golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer, no regex over HTML structure) plus stdlib encoding/json for embedded structured data. Detection runs three strategies in strict priority order. (1) schema.org Person via JSON-LD: every <script type="application/ld+json"> block is parsed and walked recursively — top-level Person, Person inside an @graph or array, and Person under an Organization's member/employee/founder/alumni properties are all harvested, with namespaced @type forms (https://schema.org/Person, schema:Person) recognised; jobTitle, description, image (string / array / ImageObject.url), sameAs and url become role/bio/photo_url/links; results are de-duplicated by name. (2) schema.org microdata: elements whose itemtype ends in /Person are read via their itemprop children (name/jobTitle/description/image/sameAs/url), without descending into nested itemscope entities. (3) Conservative repeated-card heuristic: under a common parent it finds the dominant run (>=2) of same-tag card containers (li/article/div/section), extracts a name from the first heading or strong/b that passes a strict person-name gate (2-4 tokens, each capitalised or an allowed name connective, no digits/role-words, length-capped), a role from a class/itemprop hint (role/title/position/jobTitle) or a nearby short line carrying a known role keyword, the longest paragraph as bio, the first img as photo, and the card's anchor hrefs as links. The heuristic is deliberately conservative: a group must yield >=2 qualifying members AND at least one must carry a role line, otherwise it is dropped — so navigation bars, footers, and link lists are not emitted as people. Photo and link hrefs are resolved against the fetched final URL via net/url ResolveReference; javascript:/fragment-only hrefs are dropped. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch) in plain-HTML render mode (the static DOM is sufficient — no JS rendering needed). SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) plus localhost/.local names, with cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards catching DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode scans pasted HTML with no fetch. Non-fatal conditions are pushed to degraded[] (fetch_failed:<err>, no_extractable_text, jsonld_parse_error, heuristic_fallback, no_members) instead of failing; a page with text but no detectable roster still returns 200, and the response reports which method (jsonld/microdata/heuristic/none) was used. ~35 unit tests cover JSON-LD single/array/@graph/org-employee/namespaced-type/ImageObject/given-family-name/malformed/dedup/precedence, microdata single/multiple/content-attr, the heuristic across div/li/article cards with role-evidence gating, single-card rejection, nav rejection, mixed-role majority, bio capture, relative-link resolution, the name gate's accept/reject sets, omitempty JSON shape, non-nil slice stability, script isolation, and SSRF rejection. /selftest exercises all three pure-logic strategies plus a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal. TRL-4 ceiling: JSON-LD and microdata paths are exact, but the heuristic card detection is structurally fragile against unmarked, bespoke, or visually-driven layouts — distinguishing a true team card from a generic content card without semantic markup is a judgement a trained model would make far better than these hand-tuned rules, and the conservative gate trades recall (missing people) for precision (not emitting non-people).

testimonial-extractor.description: Extracts testimonials / customer reviews / endorsements from a web page or raw HTML — schema.org Review JSON-LD first, then <blockquote> with <cite>/<figcaption> attribution, then heuristic testimonial cards — capturing each testimonial's text, author, author_title, numeric rating (when present), and source_url, plus count and a has_ratings flag.
testimonial-extractor.trl_evidence: Real DOM walk over a fetched HTML page (golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer; zero regex for structure) with a confidence-ordered three-strategy extractor. Strategy 1 (authoritative): parses every <script type=application/ld+json> block with the stdlib JSON decoder and harvests schema.org Review / Testimonial nodes — recursing through @graph, arrays, and the review/reviews/itemListElement properties of a parent Product/Organization — mapping reviewBody/description/text to the body, a string-or-Person/Organization author to name + jobTitle/affiliation/worksFor, reviewRating.ratingValue (or a bare ratingValue, numeric or string) to the rating, and url/sameAs to a source link. Strategy 2: <blockquote> testimonials attributed by a nested <cite>, an enclosing <figure>'s <figcaption>, or an immediately-following <cite>; a bare unattributed pull-quote is conservatively rejected UNLESS it sits inside a testimonial-flavoured container, to avoid emitting marketing/nav copy. Strategy 3 (best-effort, flagged heuristic_fallback): block-container cards (div/section/article/li/figure) whose class/id mentions testimonial/review/endorsement/quote/feedback, taking the longest quote element/paragraph as the body and a cite/footer/author-class/shortest-paragraph line as the attribution; an outer wrapper that contains inner card containers descends to those rather than emitting one blob. Ratings are best-effort and conservative: schema.org microdata itemprop=ratingValue (content= or text) first, then aria-label/title phrases like '5 out of 5' / 'Rated 4.5' / '4.5/5', then a filled-star glyph count — surfaced per-testimonial as an omitempty numeric rating plus a top-level has_ratings flag. JSON-LD short-circuits the DOM heuristics so the same visible card is never double-counted. Author lines are split into author + author_title on a leading dash/em-dash and the first comma or ' - '/' – ' separator. Relative source/author URLs are resolved against the final fetched URL. Input is fetched through the fleet HTTP fetch cache (go-common/fleetfetch, plain-HTML render mode) with SSRF defense-in-depth: input-time block of literal loopback/private/link-local IPs (safehttp block list) plus localhost/.local names, and cache-side + safehttp-fallback dial-time guards for DNS-resolved private IPs. Direct text= mode scans pasted HTML with no fetch. Non-fatal conditions go to a non-nil degraded[] (fetch_failed:<err>, no_extractable_text, jsonld_parse_error, heuristic_fallback, no_testimonials) instead of failing; a page with text but no testimonials still returns 200. ~40 unit tests plus /selftest cover the JSON-LD review/rating/author paths, blockquote+cite/figcaption/following-cite attribution, the conservative bare-quote rejection, heuristic card extraction and wrapper de-duplication, all three rating heuristics and has_ratings, author-line splitting, empty/garbage/no-testimonial input, parse-input aliases, missing-param 400, SSRF rejection, and the text-mode handler paths; /selftest also runs a live example.com fetch through the cache so the deploy smoke-gate has a real signal. TRL-4 ceiling vs higher: Strategy 1 is exact, but the heuristic-card path and star-glyph/aria rating detection are structurally fragile — they pattern-match on author-supplied class names and rating glyphs, so an unconventional widget will be missed or mis-rated; lifting that ceiling would need site-specific templates or NLP-grade layout inference, not more heuristics.

tos-finder.trl_ceiling_reason: No JS render engine: JS-rendered SPA footers (e.g. naver.com) carry no static legal anchors and stay unreachable for a CPU-only pure-Go fetcher; plus no persistence / cross-source corroboration. Closing either needs a headless browser or a stored evidence store, out of scope for this service shape.
tos-finder.trl_evidence: Link-scan + canonical-path probe + content-verified legal-doc finder (ToS/Privacy/Cookie/DMCA/AUP/GDPR-DPA/Imprint/Refund) with per-pattern regex gazetteers, confidence bands, soft-404 rejection, and real table-driven tests. Pass-3 closed a structural geographic bias: the Pass-1/2 gazetteer was English/Latin-only AND used Go's regexp \b word boundary, which is ASCII-only and therefore never anchors a CJK/Cyrillic phrase even if a token were added. Added a per-pattern scriptRegex (no-\b substring alternation) covering ja/zh-simp/zh-trad/ko/ru/uk/el/tr/ar/th/hi/vi/id-ms legal-doc anchor text (real anchors lifted from qq.com/tencent.com footers), a matchTextOrScript fusion point wired into link-text classification, title/h1 confirmation, and the canonical-probe FP guard; a scriptLegalVocabRE corroborator so geo coverage does not relax precision; CJK/Cyrillic soft-404 strings; and IDN U-label->A-label normalisation via golang.org/x/net/idna (IDNA2008 lookup, already a transitive dep, CGO-free). domain_legal_docs stores only the aggregate envelope (verdict/documents_found/present[]/missing[]/imprint_present) with no per-doc URLs, so FP hunting was done by re-running deployed 1.2.1 live on a geo-diverse subset. 33 new multilingual fixture tests green; go build/vet/gofmt clean. Live re-measure on deployed 1.3.0: qq.com none->comprehensive (服务协议->terms_of_service, 隐私政策->privacy_policy via link-text match), ok.ru none->partial (privacy+cookie); naver.com stays none (JS-SPA render limit, links_scanned 0) and tencent.com/rakuten.co.jp unknown (upstream RenderJS/fetch-cache 504 flake this run) for reasons outside a CPU-only gazetteer.

trust-decay.trl_evidence: v2.3.1 replaced the English-only copyright regex with a country-agnostic extractor: 25 localized reserved-rights markers (CJK/Korean/Cyrillic/Romance/Germanic/Nordic/Slavic), CJK YYYY年 + full-width years, marker-straddling window (40 before/60 after) with a <script>/<style> boundary stop and \b year word-boundaries (so 2055 inside an Italian Codice Fiscale 91074220558 is never a year); latest-year-wins fixes markup-split ranges. Bare (c) dropped as a marker (it let a (c)AdOcean 2003-2025 ad-script hijack context.ro's real © 2022). url-shortener.trl_ceiling_reason: needs real browser DOM for URL preview
url-shortener.trl_evidence: Multi-step detection with tests, SSRF guards, verdict structure

user-demographics.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-fetch homepage inference cannot reach production-grade (TRL 8+) audience certainty without persistence/cross-checks; also gated by the shared fleetfetch tier (RenderJS 502 fleet-wide).
user-demographics.trl_evidence: Pass-3 added signal_multilingual.go: per-language tone/income/professional lexicons for 13 locales (DE/FR/ES/PT/IT/NL/PL/RU/TR/JA/KO/ZH/AR), firing only as a fallback when the English detector is empty and language is non-English (en path byte-for-byte unchanged, regression-guarded). Fixture-layer before->after on 5 localized homepages: inferred-family fires 0->11, confidence low->high; existing eval precision/recall floors still 1.00. Live before->after fetch-blocked (RenderJS 502 fleet-wide; stripe.com control returned 0 pages), so delta proven at unit/fixture layer per playbook.

utils-readcontent.name: Read Content
utils-readcontent.trl_evidence: 9/10 corpus PASS (1 BROKEN: news.ycombinator.com returns 500 — pre-existing edge case, not regression). Extracts structured article content from real-world sites across SaaS landing pages, news sites, government, blogs.

vendor-security-scorecard.description: Third-party / vendor risk scorecard: HTTP security headers, HSTS, cookie flags, DNSSEC, CORS posture, end-of-life tech — 6 security analyzers merged into one graded report.
vendor-security-scorecard.trl_ceiling_reason: Reaches TRL 8 with CVSS-weighted aggregate score and remediation guidance.
vendor-security-scorecard.trl_evidence: 6/6 OK on stripe.com and github.com; hsts A+, security_headers B/73. Public at vendor-security-scorecard.0crawl.com.

video-content.description: Video-content detector: schema.org VideoObject parsing, platform detection (YouTube/Vimeo/Wistia), autoplay + accessibility scoring.
video-content.trl_ceiling_reason: needs ML model for codec and bitrate inference
video-content.trl_evidence: RFC-compliant video detection with tests, SSRF guards, verdict structure

voice-search.trl_evidence: Curated voice-search SEO audit combines structured-data, question/answer, conversational-copy, mobile, and page signals with an explainable score. It remains developing pending broader fixtures and cross-engine validation.

wayback-checker.trl_evidence: CDX API integration, snapshot distribution analysis, historical HTTPS adoption trends.

web-vitals-report.description: Core Web Vitals and performance report: page-load metrics, CrUX field vitals (p75 LCP/CLS/INP/TTFB), DOM complexity, compression, cache policy, website carbon footprint.
web-vitals-report.trl_ceiling_reason: Reaches TRL 7 with weighted performance grade.
web-vitals-report.trl_evidence: 6/6 OK on stripe.com; page_load slow + carbon C. Public at web-vitals-report.0crawl.com.

website-carbon.trl_ceiling_reason: needs authenticated session for extended carbon source data
website-carbon.trl_evidence: Sustainable Web Design Model v3 / v4 implementation: per-segment kWh (data centre 0.81 + network 0.65 + device 0.55) × configurable grid intensity (default 442 gCO2/kWh, ?intensity= override). First-visit vs returning-visitor blend (returning_ratio default 0.25, cache factor 0.25). Sub-resource discovery walks /

websocket-analyzer.trl_evidence: 4 probes (origin/auth/subprotocol/framesize) with hard 1MiB cap, in-process WS fixtures for /selftest, safehttpAllowLoopback() toggle for test-time SSRF guard

wellknown-scanner.trl_evidence: All RFC 8615 paths probed with response classification.

wp-plugins.trl_ceiling_reason: Vulnerability enrichment needs a maintained plugin CVE/WPScan-style dataset.
wp-plugins.trl_evidence: WordPress fingerprinting covers generator/header/assets, plugin/theme versions, endpoint exposure, confidence scoring, and unit tests.

wps.trl_ceiling_reason: Lab-grade Core Web Vitals require real browser timings rather than static analysis.
wps.trl_evidence: Performance score combines transport, compression, render blockers, image formats, cache headers, resource hints, asset sampling, and tests.

xss-payload.trl_evidence: security tooling, payload generation and validation

xss-scanner.trl_ceiling_reason: Real XSS confirmation requires DOM canary injection (headless browser). Reflection regex isn't real XSS.
